1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: HAS NEWS OF the Overpowering Event reached you?

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Certainly the news of the calamity that will overwhelm has come to you!

1.author: Arberry
1.text: Hast thou received the story of the Enveloper?

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: Hath there come Unto thee the story of the Enveloping Event?

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Has there come to you the narration of the overwhelming (i.e. the Day of Resurrection);

1.author: Itani
1.text: Has there come to you the news of the overwhelming?

1.author: Maududi
1.text: Has the news of the overwhelming event reached you?

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: Has there come to you the narration of Al-Ghashiyah (the overwhelming)

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: Hath there come unto thee tidings of the Overwhelming?

1.author: Qarai
1.text: Did you receive the account of the Enveloper?

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: Have you received the news of the Enveloper?

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: Has there reached you the report of the Overwhelming [event]?

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: Have you heard the story of the overwhelming event (the Day of Judgment)?

1.author: Shakir
1.text: Has not there come to you the news of the overwhelming calamity?

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: Have you heard about the Overwhelming Event?

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: Has the story reached thee of the overwhelming (Event)?

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: Many faces will be contrite on that day,

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: Many a face will be disgraced on that day.

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: Faces on that Day shall be downcast,

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: Some faces, that Day, will be humiliated (in the Hell-fire, i.e. the faces of all disbelievers, Jews and Christians, etc.).

2.author: Itani
2.text: Faces on that Day will be shamed.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: Some faces that Day shall be downcast with fear,

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: Some faces that Day will be Khashi`ah.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: On that day (many) faces will be downcast,

2.author: Qarai
2.text: Some faces on that day will be humbled,

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: On that Day faces shall be humbled,

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: [Some] faces, that Day, will be humbled,

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: On that day the faces of some people will be humbly cast down,

2.author: Shakir
2.text: (Some) faces on that day shall be downcast,

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: On that Day, there shall be downcast faces,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: Some faces, that Day, will be humiliated,

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: Labouring (hard in the worldly life by worshipping others besides Allah), weary (in the Hereafter with humility and disgrace).

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: troubled and tired as a result of their deeds in the past.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: They shall roast into the scalding Fire,

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: They will enter in the hot blazing Fire,

4.author: Maududi
4.text: they shall burn in a Scorching Fire;

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: They will enter into Fire, Hamiyah.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: they will enter a scorching fire

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: They will [enter to] burn in an intensely hot Fire.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: They will suffer the heat of the blazing fire

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: they shall enter a burning Fire

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: The while they enter the Blazing Fire,-

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: Given water from the boiling spring to drink.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: Made to drink water from the boiling hot spring.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Given to drink of a spring fiercely boiling.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: They will be given to drink from a boiling spring,

5.author: Itani
5.text: Given to drink from a flaming spring.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: their drink shall be from a boiling spring.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: They will be given to drink from a boiling (Aniyah) spring,

5.author: Qarai
5.text: and made to drink from a boiling spring.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: that is fueled from a tremendously hot fountain,

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: They will be given drink from a boiling spring.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: and will be made to drink from a fiercely boiling spring.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Made to drink from a boiling spring.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: and will be made to drink from a boiling spring,

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: The while they are given, to drink, of a boiling hot spring,

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: They will have no food except bitter thorn,

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: There is no food for them except thorns of fire.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: no food for them but cactus thorn

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: No food shall be theirs save bitter thorn,

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: No food will there be for them but a poisonous thorny plant,

6.author: Itani
6.text: They will have no food except thorns.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: They shall have no food except bitter dry thorns

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: No food will there be for them but from Dari`,

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: No food for them save bitter thorn-fruit

6.author: Qarai
6.text: They will have no food except cactus,

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: no food for them except thorny plants

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: For them there will be no food except from a poisonous, thorny plant

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: They will have no food other than bitter and thorny fruit

6.author: Shakir
6.text: They shall have no food but of thorns,

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: they shall have no food but thorns,

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: No food will there be for them but a bitter Dhari'

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Laysa lahum taAAamun illa min dareeAAin

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: Which neither give strength nor satisfy the hunger.

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: Which shall neither nourish nor avail against hunger.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: Which will neither nourish nor avail against hunger.

7.author: Itani
7.text: That neither nourishes, nor satisfies hunger.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: that will neither nourish nor satisfy their hunger.

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: Which will neither nourish nor avail against hunger.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: Which doth not nourish nor release from hunger.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: neither nourishing, nor of avail against hunger.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: which neither sustains, nor satisfy hunger.

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: Which neither nourishes nor avails against hunger.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: which will neither fatten them nor satisfy them.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: Which will neither fatten nor avail against hunger.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: which will neither nourish nor satisfy hunger.

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: Which will neither nourish nor satisfy hunger.

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: La yusminu wala yughnee min jooAAin

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: Many faces will be joyous on that day,

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: Many a face will be in serenity on that day.

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Faces on that Day shall be de lighted,

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: (Other) faces, that Day, will be joyful,

8.author: Itani
8.text: Faces on that Day will be joyful.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: On that very Day some faces shall be radiant with joy,

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: Faces that Day will be joyful,

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: In that day other faces will be calm,

8.author: Qarai
8.text: Some faces on that day will be joyous,

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: (Whereas other) faces on that Day will be joyful,

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: [Other] faces, that Day, will show pleasure.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: However, on that day the faces of other people will be happy.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: (Other) faces on that day shall be happy,

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: Some faces on that Day will be radiant,

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: (Other) faces that Day will be joyful,

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: Glad with their endeavour (for their good deeds which they did in this world, along with the true Faith of Islamic Monotheism).

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: and pleased with the result of their deeds in the past.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: well pleased with the result of their striving,

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: They shall be in a Garden lofty:

10.author: Maududi
10.text: They will be in a lofty Garden

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: They will live in an exalted garden

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: In which they will not hear any lewd speech.

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: They shall hear therein no vain discourse;

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: Where they shall neither hear harmful speech nor falsehood,

11.author: Itani
11.text: In it you will hear no nonsense.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: wherein they shall hear no vain talk.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: Where they shall neither hear harmful speech nor falsehood.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Where they hear no idle speech,

11.author: Qarai
11.text: where they will not hear any vain talk.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: where they will hear no idle talk.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: Wherein they will hear no unsuitable speech.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: wherein they will not hear any vain talk.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: Wherein you shall not hear vain talk.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: where they will hear no idle talk,

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: Where they shall hear no (word) of vanity:

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: There is a stream of running water in it;

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: In it is a flowing spring.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: Therein shall be a spring running!

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: Therein will be a running spring,

12.author: Itani
12.text: In it is a flowing spring.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: In it there shall be a flowing spring,

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: Therein will be a running spring.

12.author: Qarai
12.text: In it is a flowing spring

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: A gushing fountain shall be there

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: Within it is a flowing spring.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: Therein will be a flowing spring,

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: Therein will be a bubbling spring:

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: And within it are couches placed on high,

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: Therein will be thrones raised high,

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: Therein will be thrones raised high.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: Within it are couches raised high

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: Therein will be Thrones (of dignity), raised on high,

15.author: Qarai
15.text: and cushions laid out in an array,

15.author: Shakir
15.text: And cushions set in a row,

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: Do they not then ponder how the clouds were formed;

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: So do they not see the camel – how it has been created?

17.author: Arberry
17.text: What, do they not consider how the camel was created,

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Look they not at the camels, how they are created?

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: Do they not look at the camels, how they are created?

17.author: Itani
17.text: Do they not look at the camels—how they are created?

17.author: Maududi
17.text: Do (these unbelievers) not observe the camels: how they were created?

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: Do they not look at the camels, how they are created

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: Will they not regard the camels, how they are created?

17.author: Qarai
17.text: Do they not observe the camel, [to see] how it has been created?

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: What, do they not reflect upon how the camel was created?

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: Then do they not look at the camels - how they are created?

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: Have they not looked at how the camel is created,

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Will they not then consider the camels, how they are created?

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: Do they never reflect on the camels and how they were created,

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: Do they not look at the Camels, how they are made?-

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Afala yanthuroona ila alibili kayfa khuliqat

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: And the heavens, how it was raised high;

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: And the heaven – how it has been raised?

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: And at the heaven, how it is raised?

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: And at the heaven, how it is raised?

18.author: Itani
18.text: And at the sky—how it is raised?

18.author: Maududi
18.text: And the sky: how it was raised high?

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: And at the heaven, how it is raised

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: And the heaven, how it is raised?

18.author: Qarai
18.text: and the sky, how it has been raised?

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: And how the heaven was raised up,

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: And at the sky - how it is raised?

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: how the heavens are raised up high,

18.author: Shakir
18.text: And the heaven, how it is reared aloft,

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: and on the sky, how it is raised aloft,

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: And at the Sky, how it is raised high?-

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: And the mountains, how they were fixed;

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: And the mountains – how they have been established?

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: And at the mountains, how they are rooted?

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: And at the mountains, how they are rooted and fixed firm?

19.author: Itani
19.text: And at the mountains—how they are installed?

19.author: Maududi
19.text: And the mountains: how they were fixed?

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: And at the mountains, how they are rooted

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: And the hills, how they are set up?

19.author: Qarai
19.text: and the mountains, how they have been set?

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: and how the mountains were firmly fixed?

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: And at the mountains - how they are erected?

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: how the mountains are set firm,

19.author: Shakir
19.text: And the mountains, how they are firmly fixed,

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: and on the mountains, how they are firmly set up,

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: And at the Mountains, how they are fixed firm?-

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: And the earth, how it was spread out?

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: And the earth – how it has been spread out?

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: And at the earth, how it is outspread?

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: And at the earth, how it is spread out?

20.author: Itani
20.text: And at the earth—how it is spread out?

20.author: Maududi
20.text: And the earth: how it was spread out?

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: And at the earth, how it is outspread

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: And the earth, how it is spread?

20.author: Qarai
20.text: and the earth, how it has been surfaced?

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: And how the earth was outstretched?

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: And at the earth - how it is spread out?

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: and how the earth is spread out?

20.author: Shakir
20.text: And the earth, how it is made a vast expanse?

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: and on the earth, how it is spread out?

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: And at the Earth, how it is spread out?

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: Remind them; you are surely a reminder.

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: Therefore advise; indeed you are a proclaimer of advice. (The Holy Prophet is a Remembrance from Allah.)

21.author: Arberry
21.text: Then remind them! Thou art only a reminder;

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: Admonish thou then; thou art but an admonisher.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: So remind them (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)), you are only a one who reminds.

21.author: Itani
21.text: So remind. You are only a reminder.

21.author: Maududi
21.text: So render good counsel, for you are simply required to counsel,

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: So remind them -- you are only one who reminds.

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: Remind them, for thou art but a remembrancer,

21.author: Qarai
21.text: So admonish—for you are only an admonisher,

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: Therefore remind, you are only a Reminder.

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: So remind, [O Muhammad]; you are only a reminder.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: (Muhammad), preach; you are only a preacher.

21.author: Shakir
21.text: Therefore do remind, for you are only a reminder.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: So, [O Prophet] exhort them: your task is only to exhort,

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: Therefore do thou give admonition, for thou art one to admonish.

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: You are not a warden over them,

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: You are not at all a guardian over them.

22.author: Arberry
22.text: thou art not charged to oversee them.

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: Thou art not over them a warden.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: You are not a dictator over them.

22.author: Itani
22.text: You have no control over them.

22.author: Maududi
22.text: and are not invested with the authority to compel them.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: You are not a Musaytir over them.

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: Thou art not at all a warder over them.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: and not a taskmaster over them—

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: You are not charged to oversee them.

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: You are not over them a controller.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: You do not have full control over them.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: You are not a watcher over them;

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: Thou art not one to manage (men's) affairs.

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: Other than him who turns his back and denies,

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: Except whoever turns away and disbelieves –

23.author: Arberry
23.text: But he who turns his back, and disbelieves,

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: But whosoever will turn back and disbelieve -

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: Save the one who turns away and disbelieves

23.author: Itani
23.text: But whoever turns away and disbelieves.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: But whoever will turn away (from the Truth),

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: Save the one who turns away and disbelieves.

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: But whoso is averse and disbelieveth,

23.author: Qarai
23.text: except him who turns back and disbelieves.

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: As for those who turn their back and disbelieve,

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: However, he who turns away and disbelieves -

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: However, those who turn away and disbelieve,

23.author: Shakir
23.text: But whoever turns back and disbelieves,

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: But whoever turns back and denies the truth,

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: But if any turn away and reject Allah,-

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: In which case he will be punished by God with the severest punishment.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: So Allah will mete out to him the greatest punishment.

24.author: Arberry
24.text: God shall chastise him with the greatest chastisement.

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Him Allah shall torment with the greatest torment.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: Then Allah will punish him with the greatest punishment.

24.author: Itani
24.text: God will punish him with the greatest punishment.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: Allah will chastise him with the most terrible chastisement.

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: Then Allah will punish him with the greatest punishment.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: Allah will punish him with direst punishment.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: Him Allah will punish with the greatest punishment.

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: Allah will punish them with the greatest punishment.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: Then Allah will punish him with the greatest punishment.

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: God will punish them with the greatest torment.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: Allah will chastise him with the greatest chastisement.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: will be punished by God with the greatest punishment.

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: Allah will punish him with a mighty Punishment,

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: To Us is surely their returning;

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: Indeed only towards Us is their return

25.author: Arberry
25.text: Truly, to Us is their return;

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: Verily Unto us is their reckoning.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: Verily, to Us will be their return;

25.author: Maududi
25.text: Surely to Us is their return;

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: Verily, to Us will be their return;

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: Lo! unto Us is their return

25.author: Qarai
25.text: Indeed to Us will be their return.

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: Indeed, to Us they shall return,

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: Indeed, to Us is their return.

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: To Us they will all return.

25.author: Shakir
25.text: Surely to Us is their turning back,

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: Certainly, it is to Us that they will return.

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: For to Us will be their return;

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: Ours is surely then to reckon with them.

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: Then indeed only upon Us is their reckoning.

26.author: Arberry
26.text: then upon Us shall rest their reckoning.

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: Then verily on us is their reckoning.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: Then verily, for Us will be their reckoning.

26.author: Itani
26.text: Then upon Us rests their reckoning.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: and then it is for Us to call them to account.

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: Then verily, for Us will be their reckoning.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: Then, indeed, their reckoning will lie with Us.

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: then upon Us shall rest their reckoning.

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: Then indeed, upon Us is their account.

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: In Our hands are their accounts.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: Then surely upon Us is the taking of their account.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: Then, surely, it is for Us to call them to account.

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: Then it will be for Us to call them to account.
